queue_item.action: Classify whether browser performance observations are complete enough to support bounded absence claims.

progress.finding: No observed bad entry is not evidence of absence unless support is established, at least one callback ran, and droppedEntriesCount is zero; buffered capture remains bounded.
progress.verification: 6 deterministic Node.js tests passed in a local isolated runtime using the exact committed source and test content.
progress.privacy_boundary: Aggregate support and count fields only; no raw performance entries, DOM targets, URLs, scripts, private text, or health data.

queue_item.handoff: Vercel Studio should attach this classification to R-005 through R-007 evidence. Treat unsupported, unconfirmed, or known_incomplete observations as incapable of proving that no performance defect occurred.

coordination.reason: Append-only update avoids overwriting concurrent team state while ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is stale relative to recent commits.
